title: Anda Masala Curry Dhaba Style Recipe | ढाबे वाली अंडा मसाला करी | Chef Sanjyot Keer
ingredients: • Ghee 1 tbsp | • Boiled eggs 8 nos. | • Kashmiri red chilli powder a pinch | • Haldi powder a pinch | • Salt to taste | • Ghee 2 tbsp + oil 1 tbsp | 1. Jeera 1 tsp | 2. Dalchini 1 inch | 3. Green cardamom 2-3 pods | 4. Black cardamom 1 no. | 5. Tej patta 1 no. | • Onions 5 medium size / 400 gm (chopped) | • Ginger garlic chilli ½ cup (roughly chopped) | 1. Turmeric powder ½ tsp | 2. Spicy red chilli powder 2 tsp | 3. Kashmiri red chilli powder 1 tbsp | 4. Coriander powder 2 tbsp | 5. Jeera powder 1 tsp | • Tomatoes 4 medium size (chopped) | • Salt to taste | • Garam masala 1 tsp | • Kasuri methi 1 tsp | • Ginger 1 inch (julienned) | • Green chillies 2-3 nos. (slit) | • Fresh coriander a small handful
instructions: • Set a pan on medium heat, add ghee, boiled eggs, red chilli powder, haldi & salt, stir & shallow fry the eggs for couple of minutes.` keep the shallow fried eggs aside to be used later. | • For curry, set a wok on medium heat, add ghee & the whole spices, stir & further add chopped onions, stir and cook until the onions turn golden brown in colour. | • Add the roughly chopped ginger garlic chilli, stir & cook for 3-4 minutes on medium flame. | • Further lower the flame and add the powdered spices, mix well & add some hot water to avoid the spices from burning. | • Increase the flame to medium heat, stir & cook until the ghee is released. | • Now, add the tomatoes & salt, stir & cook well for at least 8-10 minutes or until the tomatoes has mixed well with the masala. | • Add some hot water, stir & cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes. | • Now, add the shallow fried eggs, stir & cook on high flame for 5-6 minutes. | • Now add ginger, green chillies, kasuri methi, garam masala & freshly chopped coriander leaves, stir well. | • You can adjust the consistency of the gravy by adding hot water as required, your dhaba style egg curry is ready, serve hot with some tandoori roti or any Indian bread of your choice.
raw_text: Full written recipe for Dhaba style egg curry Prep time: 15-20 minutes Cooking time: 25-30 minutes Serves: 3-4 people Ingredients: Fried eggs: • Ghee 1 tbsp • Boiled eggs 8 nos. • Kashmiri red chilli powder a pinch • Haldi powder a pinch • Salt to taste For curry: • Ghee 2 tbsp + oil 1 tbsp • Whole spices: 1. Jeera 1 tsp 2. Dalchini 1 inch 3. Green cardamom 2-3 pods 4. Black cardamom 1 no. 5. Tej patta 1 no. • Onions 5 medium size / 400 gm (chopped) • Ginger garlic chilli ½ cup (roughly chopped) • Powdered spices: 1. Turmeric powder ½ tsp 2. Spicy red chilli powder 2 tsp 3. Kashmiri red chilli powder 1 tbsp 4. Coriander powder 2 tbsp 5. Jeera powder 1 tsp • Tomatoes 4 medium size (chopped) • Salt to taste • Garam masala 1 tsp • Kasuri methi 1 tsp • Ginger 1 inch (julienned) • Green chillies 2-3 nos. (slit) • Fresh coriander a small handful Method: • Set a pan on medium heat, add ghee, boiled eggs, red chilli powder, haldi & salt, stir & shallow fry the eggs for couple of minutes.` keep the shallow fried eggs aside to be used later. • For curry, set a wok on medium heat, add ghee & the whole spices, stir & further add chopped onions, stir and cook until the onions turn golden brown in colour. • Add the roughly chopped ginger garlic chilli, stir & cook for 3-4 minutes on medium flame. • Further lower the flame and add the powdered spices, mix well & add some hot water to avoid the spices from burning. • Increase the flame to medium heat, stir & cook until the ghee is released. • Now, add the tomatoes & salt, stir & cook well for at least 8-10 minutes or until the tomatoes has mixed well with the masala. • Add some hot water, stir & cook on medium heat for 2-3 minutes. • Now, add the shallow fried eggs, stir & cook on high flame for 5-6 minutes. • Now add ginger, green chillies, kasuri methi, garam masala & freshly chopped coriander leaves, stir well. • You can adjust the consistency of the gravy by adding hot water as required, your dhaba style egg curry is ready, serve hot with some tandoori roti or any Indian bread of your choice. #YFL #SanjyotKeer #AndaMasalaCurry The Music I use in All my videos - https://bit.ly/3CdPbSc (Best for independent creators) Follow us on all platforms: Facebook - https://bit.ly/3o6alLt Instagram - https://bit.ly/2RFkL8z Follow my personal handles here: (Chef Sanjyot Keer) Facebook - https://bit.ly/3tpjqQA​​​​​​​​​ Instagram - https://bit.ly/3o0FZK8 Twitter - https://bit.ly/33qQpJN

ingredients: Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार | 2 tsp Vinegar, सिरका | 7-8 Eggs, अंडे | 1 ½ tbsp Oil, तेल | 2 Garlic cloves, roughly sliced, लहसुन | 1 inch Ginger, peeled, roughly sliced, अदरक | 3 medium Onions, roughly chopped, प्याज | 1 tbsp Degi red chili powder, देगी लाल मिर्च पाउडर | 2 medium size Tomato, roughly chopped, टमाटर | Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार | 1 heaped tbsp Coriander powder, धनिया पाउडर | ¼ cup Water, पानी | Prepared Gravy, तैयार की हुई ग्रेवी | 1 tbsp Coriander leaves, chopped, धनिया पत्ता | 2 tsp Oil, तेल | 7-8 hard-boiled Eggs, अंडे | Salt to taste, नमक स्वादअनुसार | 1 tsp Degi red chili powder, देगी लाल मिर्च पाउडर

Cook it for 10-12 minutes or until hard boiled. Transfer it to chilled water and remove the shell. Keep it aside for further use. For Gravy In a heavy bottom pan or a kadai, add oil, once it's hot, add garlic, ginger, onions and saute for 3-4 minutes or until the onions are translucent. Add degi red chili powder, tomatoes, salt to taste and mix well. Add some water, salt to taste, coriander powder and saute for a minute. Cover it with the lid and cook until the tomatoes are mushy. Transfer it to a grinder jar and grind it into a smooth paste. For Finishing Gravy In the same kadai, add water, prepared gravy and let it simmer. Add coriander leaves, prepared fried eggs and cook for 4-5 minutes or until the gravy thickens. Transfer it to a serving dish and garnish it with coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti or rice. For Frying Eggs In a pan, add oil, once it’s hot, add hard boiled eggs and toss it. Add salt to taste, degi red chili powder and toss well. Turn off the flames and keep it aside for further use. *********************** For more fantastic recipes, check out the Ranveer Brar App: 📲𝐀𝐧𝐝𝐫𝐨𝐢𝐝 - http://bit.ly/RBAppAndroid 📲𝗶𝗢𝗦 - http://bit.ly/RBAppiOS *********************** 🌎 Follow Ranveer Brar here too: ➡️ https://www.ranveerbrar.com ➡️ https://www.facebook.com/RanveerBrar/ ➡️ https://www.instagram.com/ranveer.brar/ ➡️ https://twitter.com/ranveerbrar #eggcurryrecipe #eggrecipe #ranveerbrar #andacurry #restaurantstyleeggcurry #eggrecipes #eggcurrykaisebanatehain #dhabaandacurry #andamasalacurry #eggmasala

Fry on medium heat for around 3-4 mins and then add the chopped ginger, garlic, green chillies and the cashew nuts. | - Mix and fry on medium heat for around 1-2 mins. | - Now add 1/2 cup water and make it into a smooth paste. | - Shell the boiled eggs and make 3-4 cuts on one side. Set aside.
instructions: - Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan and add 1/2 tsp each of turmeric and Kashmiri chilli powder. | - Give a stir and add the cut boiled eggs. | - Stir and fry on low heat for around 2 mins till the eggs are golden brown in colour. | Remove the fried eggs. | - Now add 1-2 tbsp oil in the same oil left in the pan . | - Heat the oil and then add the whole spices. | - Give a stir and then add the Masala paste made earlier in the grinder. | - Cook on low heat for 3-4 mins and then add the spice powders and salt and cook on low heat for another 3-4 mins. | - Now add the fresh cream and whisked curd mix and cook on low heat for 2-3 mins till oil separates. | - You can switch off the heat before you add the curd and then switch on after giving a stir. | - Now add the crushed kasuri methi, mix and simmer on low heat for 2-3 mins till oil separates. | - Add 150 ml water and then add the fried eggs, mix lightly and cover & cook on low heat for 5 mins. | - Remove lid and add the chopped coriander leaves and little water if required. | Simmer for 1-2 mins and serve hot.
raw_text: Afghani Egg Curry | Afghani Egg Gravy Recipe | Afghani Anda Curry | Afghani Egg Korma | Afghani Egg Recipe | Afghani Anda Gravy Recipe | Anda Curry | Egg Curry | Egg Gravy | Egg Curry Recipe Shop CELA Brand of Kitchen Products by Spice Eats - https://amzn.to/4rx93bX Ingredients for Afghani Egg Curry: (Tsp-Teaspoon; Tbsp-Tablespoon) Boiled Eggs-6 nos. For frying eggs- - Oil- 1 tbsp - Turmeric powder-1/2 tsp - Kashmiri Chilli Powder-1/2 tsp For the Masala Paste: - Onions sliced- 2 medium (150 gms) - Ginger chopped - 1” (8-10 gms) - Garlic cloves-5-6 - Green chillies - 4 (12 gms) - Cashew nuts-10 - Coriander leaves-handful (10 gms) Tempering: - Green Cardamom-3 - Cloves- 3 - Cinnamon-2 Spice Powders: - Turmeric powder- pinch - Cumin powder-1/2 tsp - Black pepper powder-1/2 tsp - Garam Masala powder-1/2 tsp - Roasted & crushed Kasuri Methi- 1/2 tsp Other Ingredients: - Whisked Curd/ Yogurt- 3 tbsp - Fresh cream- 2-3 tbsp - Oil- 3 tbsp - Salt- 3/4 tsp - Coriander leaves, chopped for garnish- 2 tbsp Preparation: - To make the Masala paste, heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan and add the sliced onions. Fry on medium heat for around 3-4 mins and then add the chopped ginger, garlic, green chillies and the cashew nuts. - Mix and fry on medium heat for around 1-2 mins. - Remove onto a plate and cool it. - To make the paste, add this mixture into the grinder and coarse grind it. - Now add 1/2 cup water and make it into a smooth paste. - Shell the boiled eggs and make 3-4 cuts on one side. Set aside. Process: - Heat 1 tbsp oil in a pan and add 1/2 tsp each of turmeric and Kashmiri chilli powder. - Give a stir and add the cut boiled eggs. - Stir and fry on low heat for around 2 mins till the eggs are golden brown in colour. Remove the fried eggs. - Now add 1-2 tbsp oil in the same oil left in the pan . - Heat the oil and then add the whole spices. - Give a stir and then add the Masala paste made earlier in the grinder. - Cook on low heat for 3-4 mins and then add the spice powders and salt and cook on low heat for another 3-4 mins. - Now add the fresh cream and whisked curd mix and cook on low heat for 2-3 mins till oil separates. - You can switch off the heat before you add the curd and then switch on after giving a stir. - Now add the crushed kasuri methi, mix and simmer on low heat for 2-3 mins till oil separates. - Add 150 ml water and then add the fried eggs, mix lightly and cover & cook on low heat for 5 mins. - Remove lid and add the chopped coriander leaves and little water if required. Simmer for 1-2 mins and serve hot. #afghanieggcurry #afghanieggmasala #afghaniandacurry #eggcurry #eggmasala #afghanieggkorma #spiceeats #spiceeatsrecipes #spiceeatseggcurry

raw_text: Learn How To Cook Egg Masala Curry recipe from Chef Varun Inamdar. Make this authentic egg recipe in a simple, quick and easy method at your home and share your experience with us in the comments below. Ingredients:- 6 Hard Boiled Eggs, peeled 1/4 tsp turmeric powder 1 tbsp oil For the Gravy 2 tbsp oil 1 Bay Leaf 1 Cinnamon stick 2 nos green cardamom 1 no. Black cardamom 4 nos Cloves 2 tbsp Butter 200 gms boiled onion paste 200 gms fresh tomato puree 1tbsp ginger garlic crushed 2 tbsp Red Chilli powder 1 tsp Garam Masala 1/2 tsp Turmeric 1 tsp Kasoori Methi, crushed Salt to taste Fresh corriander leaves Method:- In a pan heat oil and add black cardamom, green cardamom, cloves, bay leaves and cinnamon stick. Once this starts crackling add in the boiled onion paste and give a quick mix. Add salt, ginger-garlic paste and let it cook for a minute on high flame or until golden brown in colour. Then add the turmeric powder, red chilli powder, garam masala powder, butter and cook till the raw flavour of spices goes away. Once the masala is cooked completely, add in the tomato puree, salt and cook on high flame for 2-3 minutes. Once the oil leaves the sides of the pan, add water as required and cook for 10 minutes with the lid on. To pan fry the hard boiled eggs, add some oil to a pan, turmeric powder and then the boiled eggs and fry the eggs on high flame until golden brown on all sides. Once the eggs are fried & ready add them to the gravy and cook it for 30-40 seconds. Add water as and if required. Egg Masala Curry is ready to eat! title: Jammy Egg Curry | Rich Indian Egg Curry with Whole Spices
description: Ingredients: •6 eggs (jammy boiled- see method below) •Oil, for browning onions and using that leftover oil for cooking as well •400 g onions, sliced
ingredients: •6 eggs (jammy boiled- see method below) | •400 g onions, sliced | •8 tbsp full-fat yogurt | •20 g garlic | •15 g ginger | •½ tsp black peppercorns | •2 bay leaves | •4 cloves | •5 green cardamoms | •1 mace blade | •Cinnamon 3-4cm long | •1.5 tsps Kashmiri red chilli powder | •2 cups warm water | •Salt, to taste
instructions: ~ To make jammy eggs: bring a pot of water to a rolling boil. | Gently lower in the eggs and boil for 8 minutes. | Transfer immediately into iced water, then peel. | ~ Heat oil in a pot and fry sliced onions until just browned at the edges. | Sprinkle a little salt. | Remove and set aside and once cooled, blend it with yogurt. | ~ In the same oil, add bay leaves, cloves, cardamom, mace, cinnamon and black pepper. | Let them sizzle and release aroma. | ~ Add ginger–garlic paste, stir until lightly golden. | ~ Add the onions and yoghurt paste to the pot along with Kashmiri red chilli powder + salt. | Cook until the colour deepens from pale red to a rich reddish-brown and oil begins to separate. | ~ Stir in 2 cups warm water. | Bring to a gentle boil. | ~ Add the jammy eggs (reserve a couple to slice for garnish). | Simmer briefly so the flavours soak in. | ~ Garnish with coriander, green chillies and the sliced eggs on top. | Serve hot with rotis or rice.

To make the masala paste, add onions, green chillies, ginger, garlic cloves, coriander leaves, mint leaves to a mixer jar and grind into a fine paste. Keep it aside. 2. Take water in a pot and boil. Add eggs and salt. Boil the eggs and remove the shells. Keep them aside. 3. To a pan, add oil and put the whole spices and shahi jeera. Saute. 4. Now add the ground masala paste, mix and cover the pan. Cook for 10 mins. 5. After 10 mins, add turmeric powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala powder, salt and mix well. 6. Add in the smoothly whisked fresh curd, mix and cook for 5 mins. 7. Pour water into the gravy and simmer for 10 mins by covering with a lid. 8. Add sugar, mix and add the slit boiled eggs. 9. Mix well, add chopped coriander leaves and cover the pan. Simmer for 5 mins. 10. Add fresh cream and mix well. 11. Creamy, rich and delicious Hyderabadi Egg Malai Curry is ready to be served hot. SALT | नमक A PINCH TURMERIC POWDER | हल्दी पाउडर A PINCH KASHMIRI RED CHILLI POWDER | कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च पाउडर A PINCH Final Seasoning ROASTED KASURI METHI POWDER | भुनी कसूरी मेथी पाउडर A PINCH GARAM MASALA | गरम मसाला A PINCH SALT | नमक IF REQUIRED FRESH CORIANDER | हरा धनिया A HANDFUL (CHOPPED Method: Take onions and roughly slice them. You can also use a slicer or chop them using a chopper. Remove the eye from the tomatoes and slice them into thick slices. Heat a vessel on high flame and add oil. Once the oil is hot, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add the sliced onions and roast on high flame, stirring continuously, until they turn golden brown and caramelized. In between, add splashes of hot water to help the onions caramelize faster without burning. Once the onions are well browned, add garlic, ginger, green chillies, fresh coriander stems, sliced tomatoes, and salt. Mix well and cook for 2–3 minutes on high flame. Add hot water as required, cover, and cook on low flame for 10–15 minutes until the tomatoes turn soft and mushy. After 10–15 minutes, uncover and switch off the flame. Let the mixture cool down completely. Once cooled, transfer the mixture to a mixer jar and grind into a fine puree using water as required. Keep the puree aside. Heat a kadhai on high flame and add ghee and oil. Once hot, add bay leaf, green cardamom, cinnamon, black cardamom, and cumin seeds. Let the whole spices crackle well. Add the prepared puree to the kadhai. Immediately add turmeric powder, Kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, and spicy red chilli powder. Mix well. Cook the masala on medium-high flame for 7–8 minutes, stirring continuously, until the oil separates and the gravy starts leaving the sides of the kadhai. Adjust the consistency by adding hot water as required. The gravy should be smooth, velvety, and medium-thick, not too thin and not too thick. Cover the kadhai, reduce the flame to low, and let the gravy simmer. For tempering the boiled eggs, heat a non-stick pan and add oil. Once hot, add the boiled eggs along with salt, turmeric powder, and Kashmiri red chilli powder. Toss the eggs gently so they get evenly coated and lightly roasted for better texture. Remove the eggs into a bowl and keep aside. Check the gravy consistency and ensure the oil has released on top. Add the tempered eggs to the gravy. Add roasted kasuri methi powder and garam masala. Mix gently. Taste and adjust salt if required. Add chopped fresh coriander and mix well. Your delicious, smooth and velvety Egg Curry is ready. Further add cumin seeds, cinnamon, green cardamom, green chilli, garlic & ginger, stir well & cook over medium high flame for a minute. Add chopped onions, stir well & cook over medium high flame until they turn golden brown. Once the onions turn golden brown, lower the flame & add turmeric powder, kashmiri red chilli powder & a splash of hot water, stir & increase the flame to medium high & cook the spices for 1-2 minutes. Further add the prepared tomato puree followed by salt & sugar, stir well & cook the gravy over medium flame until it starts leaving the surface of the pan, this process will take upto 15-20 minutes. Once you have cooked the gravy for 15-20 minutes & it stops sticking to the pan add hot water to adjust the consistency & stir well. Further add the sauteed eggs, fresh cream, butter, fresh coriander, roasted kasuri methi powder, garam masala, stir well & cook for 1-2 minutes then taste & adjust salt if required. instructions: Heat water in a pan, add salt and eggs. | Cover and boil them well. | Then peel the onion, ginger and garlic. | Then wash the onion, ginger and garlic, set aside. | Then chopped the onion and set aside. | Then chopped the tomatoes and green chilli and set aside. | Then cut the ginger into small pieces and set aside. | Then crush the garlic and ginger, set aside. | Once the egg is boiling, remove from the fire and peel and set aside. | Heat oil in a pan and add Bay leaves and fennel seeds. | Then add the chopped onion and salt and sauté it well. | Add curry powder such as red chilli powder, garam masala and turmeric powder. | Then add coriander powder and pepper powder. | Saute till the curry powders raw smell completely gone. | Then add the chopped tomatoes and sauté them well. | Pour a sufficient amount of water and mix it well. | Add some curry leaves and cook them well till the curry masala is well cooked. | Cover and cook them well. | Remove the lid and add the boiled eggs and cook for a few minutes. | Remove from fire and set aside. | Serve the tasty Egg Curry with chapati or appam. | Virgin Coconut Oil : https://villagecookingkerala.in/ | Recipe Link : https://villagecookingkerala.com | Business : villagecookings@gmail.com | Phone/ Whatsapp : 94 00 47 49 44
raw_text: Ingredients Eggs-5 nos Onion - 3 to 4 nos Tomatoes - 2 nos Garlic -5 to 6 nos Ginger - 1 nos Green chilli - 2 to 3 nos Curry leaves - 2 to 3 sprigs Bay leaves - 1 small Fennel seed - 1 tsp Star anise-1 nos Cinnamon- small pieces Red chilli powder - 11/2 tsp Turmeric powder - 1/2 tsp Pepper powder - 1 tsp Garam masala - 1 tsp Coriander powder - 1 tsp Oil - 2 tsp Salt -1 or 2 tsp Method Heat water in a pan, add salt and eggs. Cover and boil them well. Then peel the onion, ginger and garlic. Then wash the onion, ginger and garlic, set aside. Then chopped the onion and set aside. Then chopped the tomatoes and green chilli and set aside. Then cut the ginger into small pieces and set aside. Then crush the garlic and ginger, set aside. Once the egg is boiling, remove from the fire and peel and set aside. Heat oil in a pan and add Bay leaves and fennel seeds. Then add the chopped onion and salt and sauté it well. Add curry powder such as red chilli powder, garam masala and turmeric powder. Then add coriander powder and pepper powder. Saute till the curry powders raw smell completely gone. Then add the chopped tomatoes and sauté them well. Pour a sufficient amount of water and mix it well. Add some curry leaves and cook them well till the curry masala is well cooked. Cover and cook them well. Remove the lid and add the boiled eggs and cook for a few minutes. Remove from fire and set aside. Serve the tasty Egg Curry with chapati or appam. Cumin seeds- 1 tsp 2. Whole red chillies- 2 Spice Powders: - Red Chilli Powder- 1 tsp - Coriander powder- 1 tsp - Cumin powder- 1/2 tsp - Kashmiri Chilli Powder- 1/2 tsp - Garam Masala Powder- 3/4 tsp - Kasuri Methi (dry roasted & powdered)- 1 tsp Other Ingredients: - Onions, fine chopped- 2 medium (150 gms) - Ginger, fine chopped- 2 tsp - Garlic, fine chopped- 2 tsp - Green chillies, chopped- 2 nos - Fresh Tomato paste- 3 medium (200 gms) - Besan- 2 tsp to be mixed in 2 tbsp of water - Oil- 3 tbsp - Butter- 1 tbsp - Coriander leaves, chopped- 3-4 tbsp - Salt- 1 tsp Preparation: - Boil the eggs & shell them. Now heat oil in a pan, add 1/4th tsp turmeric powder , 1/4 tsp Chilli powder & give a stir. - Now add the boiled eggs and stir & fry on low heat for 1-2 mins till golden in colour. Remove and set aside. - Fine chop the onions, ginger, garlic, green chillies & coriander leaves. - Blend red & ripe tomatoes into a paste - Dry roast the Kasuri methi for 30 secs. Cool and crush it into a coarse powder. Process: - Heat 3 tbsp of oil in a kadhai. Add the cumin seeds & allow it to splutter. - Now add the red chillies and give a stir. - Now add the chopped onions and fry on medium high to medium low heat for around 8 mins till the onions are light brown in colour. Also add a pinch of salt after adding the onions. - Add in the chopped ginger, garlic & green chilies. Fry for another 2 mins on low heat. - Now add the tomato paste, mix well & then add 1 tsp of salt. - Fry/bhunno for around 5-6 mins till the tomato is totally cooked, gravy is like a smooth sauce & oil separates. - Now add the Chilli powder,coriander powder & cumin powder. - Fry for another 2-3 mins till the masala is cooked. - Now add the besan mixed in water & fry on low heat stirring continuously for 2-3 mins till the besan is cooked. - Add 200 ml water and then add the Kashmiri chilli powder, garam masala powder & Kasuri Methi. Mix well & cook for a minute. - Drop in the fried eggs, mix well, cover & cook for around 5 mins. - Garnish with coriander leaves & mix well. - Add a tbsp of butter to finish. - Serve hot with roti or rice. #dhabaeggcury #dhabaeggmasala #dhabaandacurry #eggcurry #dhabstyleeggcurry #eggmasaladhabastyle #dhabastyleegg #spiceeatsrecipes #spiceeatseggcurry

title: EGG MALAI MASALA | EGG MALAI CURRY | EGG MASALA CURRY | EGG CURRY
description: Egg Malai Curry or Egg Malai Masala is a low spice Egg Curry cooked in a creamy gravy without any cream! This unique Egg Curry was first made by Spice Eats and shared on YouTube. An easy to cook recipe that’s tasty as well as healthy. *********************** For Vegetarian Recipes follow our sister channel @SpiceSwad ***********************
ingredients: Eggs- 4 (boiled, shelled, cut in halves) | Onions - 2 medium, roughly cubed | Green chillies - 2 | Ginger-garlic paste - 1 tsp | Cumin (Zeera) powder - 1 tsp | Pepper powder - 1/2 tsp | Garam Masala powder- 1 tsp (Link to our Garam Masala Recipe: https://youtu.be/1trtejl8hwM) | Coriander leaves - 1 bunch ( finely chopped) | Milk - 200 ML | Salt - 1 tsp or to taste
instructions: - In a kadhai, add oil & once medium hot , add the onion-green chilli paste. | Lower flame & cook for 2 mins | - Now add the ginger-garlic paste & mix well | - Keep cooking in low flame , stirring often, for around 15 mins till paste changes colour | - Now switch off the flame & add the milk stirring continuously. | Mix it well with the Masala | - Switch on the flame & keep in low flame till oil separates | - Put in the cumin powder, pepper powder, garam masala powder & salt . | Mix well & cook for a few mins | - Place the egg halves in the gravy, yolk side down to absorb the spices. | - Cover & cook for few mins | - After few mins turn the egg halves yolk side up. | - Add the finely chopped coriander leaves, mix well | - Cook covered for a few mins till oil separates | - Serve, enjoy with rice or roti

raw_text: How Make A Base Gravy Masala - Base Gravy Recipe 🔔Please click on the link below and subscribe to Curries With Bumbi so that I can bring more new recipes for you- https://www.youtube.com/@UCc5O8GaMvAGjmvENEvvqZrg Ingredients Required For Making Base Gravy Recipe - *570 gm/4 large onions * 675 gm/ 6 large tomatoes * 40 gm/ 3 inch length/ 1 tablespoon grated ginger * 40 gm/ 12 large garlic cloves or about 1 & 1/2 tablespoon finely grated * 1 green chilli * 30 gm/ 15 cashew nuts soaked in 1/3 rd cup hot water * 1 & 1/2 teaspoons cumin seeds * 2 -3 dry bay leaves * 2 inch length cinnamon stick * 5 to 6 green cardamoms * 1 & 1/2 teaspoons salt or to taste * 1/2 cup oil For spice paste * 1 tsp turmeric powder * 1 tsp cumin powder * 1 tablespoon Kashmiri Chili powder (please adjust proportion according to your preference) * 1 tablespoon coriander powder * 1 tablespoon of garam masala powder * 1/3 rd cup water METHOD - * In a bowl add turmeric powder, cumin powder, Kashmiri chili powder, coriander powder and garam masala powder. Add about a third of a cup of water, mix * Heat oil over medium heat * add dry bay leaves, cinnamon stick, green cardamoms and cumin seeds. Allow the seeds to splutter to release their flavor and then add chopped onions along with a fourth of a teaspoon of salt. Stir the onions on medium heat till they turn slightly brown in color. * Add the spice paste and fry on low heat for 2 to 3 mins * Add chopped green chilli, finely grated ginger and garlic. Fry on medium heat for a few minutes until you see the oil floating on top. * add crushed tomatoes along with half a teaspoon of salt. Stir on medium heat from time to time till the tomatoes get well fried and cooked and again the oil starts leaving the sides. * make a smooth cashew nut paste in your blender for which soak some cashew nut in hot water for about an hour *Add the cashew nut paste only after you see the tomatoes and onions have got well cooked and mixed together to become one single mass * Once again when you start seeing that oil oozing out along the sides or floating on top your base gravy is done. * You can store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for about a week or you can divide them into portions and it stores well in the freezer for 6 months. title: Traditional Egg Drop Curry | Easy Coconut Egg Curry Recipe | Perfect with Rice
description: 🍳 Mangalorean Egg Drop Curry | Coastal Comfort Food 🌴 Ingredients For the Masala 4 Kashmiri red chilies
ingredients: 4 Kashmiri red chilies | 3 spicy dry red chilies | 1 tbsp coriander seeds | 1 tsp cumin seeds (jeera) | ½ tsp fenugreek seeds (methi) | ½ tsp black pepper | 1 pinch ajwain | ½ tsp oil | 1 cup grated coconut | 1 small ball tamarind | 5–6 garlic cloves | 1½ cups water | Salt to taste | ½ tsp turmeric powder | 1 finely chopped onion | 4–5 eggs | 2 green chilies
instructions: Heat oil in a pan and roast the Kashmiri chilies, spicy chilies, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, pepper, and ajwain until aromatic. | Transfer to a mixer jar. | Add grated coconut, tamarind, garlic, and a little water. | Grind into a smooth paste. | Transfer the ground masala to a kadai. | Add water, salt, turmeric powder, and chopped onion. | Bring the curry to a boil. | Reduce the flame to low and gently crack 4–5 eggs directly into the curry. | Cover and cook on low heat for 10–15 minutes, or until the eggs are cooked. | Add slit green chilies and simmer for another minute. | Serve hot with steamed rice. | If you love the bold flavors of Mangalorean cuisine, this Egg Drop Curry is for you! | Made with a rich coconut-based masala, aromatic spices, and eggs dropped directly into the simmering curry, this dish is simple yet incredibly flavorful. | Serve it with hot steamed rice for a comforting meal that brings the taste of coastal Karnataka right to your plate. | ✨ Authentic Mangalorean flavors ✨ Coconut-based curry ✨ Easy & delicious ✨ Perfect with rice | Don't forget to Like, Share & Subscribe for more Mangalorean and South Indian recipes!
raw_text: 🍳 Mangalorean Egg Drop Curry | Coastal Comfort Food 🌴 Ingredients For the Masala 4 Kashmiri red chilies 3 spicy dry red chilies 1 tbsp coriander seeds 1 tsp cumin seeds (jeera) ½ tsp fenugreek seeds (methi) ½ tsp black pepper 1 pinch ajwain ½ tsp oil For Grinding 1 cup grated coconut 1 small ball tamarind 5–6 garlic cloves Water as needed For the Curry Ground masala paste 1½ cups water Salt to taste ½ tsp turmeric powder 1 finely chopped onion 4–5 eggs 2 green chilies Method Heat oil in a pan and roast the Kashmiri chilies, spicy chilies, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, pepper, and ajwain until aromatic. Transfer to a mixer jar. Add grated coconut, tamarind, garlic, and a little water. Grind into a smooth paste. Transfer the ground masala to a kadai. Add water, salt, turmeric powder, and chopped onion. Mix well. Bring the curry to a boil. Reduce the flame to low and gently crack 4–5 eggs directly into the curry. Cover and cook on low heat for 10–15 minutes, or until the eggs are cooked. Add slit green chilies and simmer for another minute. Serve hot with steamed rice. If you love the bold flavors of Mangalorean cuisine, this Egg Drop Curry is for you! Made with a rich coconut-based masala, aromatic spices, and eggs dropped directly into the simmering curry, this dish is simple yet incredibly flavorful. Serve it with hot steamed rice for a comforting meal that brings the taste of coastal Karnataka right to your plate. ✨ Authentic Mangalorean flavors ✨ Coconut-based curry ✨ Easy & delicious ✨ Perfect with rice Don't forget to Like, Share & Subscribe for more Mangalorean and South Indian recipes!

ingredients: 1–2 tbsp oil | 2 medium onions (about 150 g), chopped | 12 garlic cloves | 2-inch piece ginger, chopped | 4–5 green chilies, chopped | 12 cashew nuts | 2 handfuls fresh coriander (cilantro), including the tender stems | 7–8 boiled eggs | 1 tsp red chili powder | 1 tsp turmeric powder | 1 tsp salt | 1 tbsp oil | 5–6 tbsp oil | 1 tbsp butter | 2 green cardamoms | 4 cloves | 4 small pieces cinnamon | Prepared green masala paste | ½ tsp black pepper powder | 1 tsp cumin powder | 1 tsp garam masala | A pinch of turmeric | 2–3 pinches salt (adjust to taste) | 4–5 tbsp yogurt (curd) | 2–3 tbsp fresh cream (or blended milk cream/malai) | 1 tbsp roasted and crushed dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi)

raw_text: #creamyeggcurry #eggcurry #andacurry #goldeneggkorma #afghaniandakorma #afghanieggcurry #eggkormarecipe #eggkorma #andakorma #eggrecipes egg korma, egg masala, egg curry, dhaba style egg korma, dhaba style anda masala recipe, cook with fem egg masala, egg gravy, anda curry, simple egg curry recipe, egg recipes, how to make egg masala, how to make eggs, homemade egg masala, homemade anda masala, cook with fem recipes, cook with fem egg korma, hyderabadi recipes, egg masala recipe in hindi, egg korma recipe in hindi, egg masala gravy, ande ka salan, malai anda, malai masala Creamy Golden Egg Curry (Soft Gravy) – English Translation Ingredients For the Green Masala Paste 1–2 tbsp oil 2 medium onions (about 150 g), chopped 12 garlic cloves 2-inch piece ginger, chopped 4–5 green chilies, chopped 12 cashew nuts A little water (for cooking and blending) 2 handfuls fresh coriander (cilantro), including the tender stems For Frying the Eggs 7–8 boiled eggs 1 tsp red chili powder 1 tsp turmeric powder 1 tsp salt 1 tbsp oil For the Curry 5–6 tbsp oil 1 tbsp butter 2 green cardamoms 4 cloves 4 small pieces cinnamon Prepared green masala paste ½ tsp black pepper powder 1 tsp cumin powder 1 tsp garam masala A pinch of turmeric 2–3 pinches salt (adjust to taste) 4–5 tbsp yogurt (curd) 2–3 tbsp fresh cream (or blended milk cream/malai) 1 tbsp roasted and crushed dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) Fresh coriander leaves for garnish Method Step 1: Make the Green Masala Paste Heat 1–2 tablespoons of oil in a pan over high heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté lightly. Add the garlic and ginger. Sauté well. Add the chopped green chilies and cashews. Continue cooking until the raw smell of the onions disappears. Add a little water, mix, cover, and cook on low heat for 3–4 minutes. Turn off the heat and add a generous amount of fresh coriander leaves along with the tender stems. Let the mixture cool, then blend into a smooth paste, adding a little water if needed. Step 2: Fry the Eggs Make a few shallow cuts on the boiled eggs. Add the eggs to the same pan. Sprinkle in the red chili powder, turmeric, salt, and oil. Mix well so the eggs are evenly coated. Fry until lightly golden, sprinkling a little water if needed during frying. Remove the eggs and set aside. Step 3: Prepare the Curry Heat 5–6 tablespoons of oil and 1 tablespoon of butter in a pan. Add the cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon. Fry until fragrant. Add the prepared green masala paste and cook over high heat, stirring frequently. (Cover the pan if it splatters.) After 2–3 minutes, add the black pepper, cumin powder, garam masala, turmeric, and salt. Continue cooking until the oil begins separating from the masala. Turn off the heat temporarily and stir in the yogurt. Mix thoroughly before turning the heat back on to prevent the yogurt from curdling. Cook until the raw smell of the yogurt disappears. Add the fresh cream (or blended malai) and cook until the oil separates again. Add the fried eggs along with any remaining spice mixture from the frying pan. Mix gently to coat the eggs. Add the roasted and crushed kasuri methi and some fresh coriander leaves. Cover and cook on low heat for 4–5 minutes. Serve Your creamy, soft, golden egg curry is ready. Turn off the heat and serve hot with naan, roti, paratha, or steamed rice. Ingredients: 4-5 Boiled Eggs 2 tbsp Oil 4-5 Cloves 1 Black Cardamom 1-2 Bay Leaves Few Green Cardamoms 1 small piece of Cinnamon 1 tsp Cumin Seeds 1/2cup chopped onions 2 tsp chopped Garlic 1 tsp chopped Ginger 1 tbsp Ginger – Garlic paste Water ½ tsp turmeric Powder 1 tsp Coriander Powder 1 tsp red Chilli Powder 1 tsp Garam Masala Powder 1 cup Tomato Puree 1 tbsp chopped Green Chillies Salt to taste Handful of Coriander Leaves 1 tsp Kasuri Methi / dried Fenugreek Leaves Method: First boil the eggs. title: SECRETS To Cooking A PERFECT Indian Style CHICKEN CURRY (STEP BY STEP INSTRUCTIONS)
description: How To Cook Indian Style Chicken Curry - Chicken Curry Recipe For Beginners If you follow this video step by step then I assure you that you will be making the best Indian style chicken curry. I tried my best to explain everything in detail and all the tips and tricks possible that go into making chicken curry. RECIPE HERE - https://bumbicurry.blogspot.com/2025/01/chicken-curry-indian-style.html Ingredients for making a chicken curry - serves 6 to 7
ingredients: * 2.2 lb/ 1 kg chicken on bones. Please note that I preferably use chicken on bones for making a chicken curry. It's not that you cannot use boneless chicken but the gravy won't be as tasty. | * 2 large potatoes quartered or medium potatoes halved. I did not boil the potatoes before adding them. | * 1 very large onion or 2 medium sized onions. I used quarter of the onion for making a paste. | * 2 inch length ginger | * 10 large garlic cloves | * 1 large tomato | * 2 green chillies (optional) | * 2 large dry bay leaf | * 1 inch length cinnamon stick | * 6 to 7 green cardamoms | * 4 to 5 cloves | * 1/4 th cup plain unflavored yogurt | * 1 teaspoon turmeric powder | * 2 heaped teaspoons Kashmiri red chilli powder. You can even use regular red chilli powder but in that case please use less. Adjust the proportion of any chilli powder according to your tolerance of heat level. Add only half a teaspoon to start with, do a taste test at the end and add more if you feel you want more chilli heat. | * 1 tablespoon ground coriander | * 1 teaspoon garam masala powder.If you want to make garam masala powder at home then here is the recipe - https://youtu.be/ciR43perZbU | * 1/4 th teaspoon ground mace (optional) | * 1/4 th teaspoon ground nutmeg (optional) | * 2 teaspoons salt (add less to start with, you can always add later) | * 1 teaspoon sugar(optional) | * 1 cup hot water. Ingredients 3 Boiled & peeled Eggs, 2 medium Onions chopped, 2 medium Tomatoes chopped, Coriander (Dhania) for granish, Curry Leaves for tempering, Ground masala- 2 inch ginger roughly diced, 2 inch Dried Coconut cut into midium slices, 1 1/2 Tbsp White Sesame seeds, 9-10 Garlic cloves. Method. Grind - Sesame seeds, dried coconut, Garlic cloves & Ginger, 1) 1st grind all the ingredients without water, 2) add 1 tbsp of water, 2 tsp of Red chilli pwd, 1 tsp Turmeric pwd, 2 tsp Garam Masala pwd, 3) Grind to a smooth paste, 4) Add a table spoon of water (If required), 5) grind untill you get a creamy texture(5-6 minutes), 6) On a medium flame add 1/2 cup oil (Groundnut oil), 7) Fry 3 Boiled eggs on a medium low heat, 8) After 5-6 minutes eggs are golden in color, 9) Transfer all 3 eggs in a bowl, 10) In the same pan add curry leaves (10-12) 11) Add chopped onions, 12) Keep stirring continuously until golden brown, 13) Add chopped tomatoes, 14) Now put the ground masala, 15) Add 1/2 cup of water for the masala to cook, 16) Keep stiring, 17) Add salt to taste, 18) Once the oil starts seperating from masala it means it is ready, 19) In a serving bowl put 2 fried eggs, 20) and transfer hot masala into it, 21) Cut the 3rd egg into half & place it on top of masala, 22) Garnish with Coriander leaves & Serve hot! 23) Egg Masala goes well with Cumin rice, Parathas or Roties. title: Easy Egg Curry Recipe | Everyday Indian Cooking | Chef Vicky Ratnani
description: This Special Egg Curry is all about comfort, balance, and honest flavours. No unnecessary gimmicks — just a well-made curry that delivers every single time. I’ve cooked this egg curry using Emami Healthy & Tasty Kacchi Ghani Mustard Oil, which adds a beautiful depth and that unmistakable desi aroma to the dish. Ingredients:
ingredients: 3-4 | Eggs | 1 tsp | Mustard seeds (Rai) | 1 tsp | Cumin seeds (Jeera) | 5-6 | Black peppercorns | 1 tsp | Carom seeds (Ajwain) | 1 | Medium-sized onion (finely chopped) | 1 tbsp | Garlic paste | 1 tsp | Dried fenugreek leaves (Kasuri methi) | 2 tsp | Kasundi mustard | 1-2 tbsp | Tomato purée | Salt (as per taste) | 1/2 cup | Coconut milk | 1 tsp | Lemon juice
instructions: Puncture the eggs lightly with a skewer or toothpick and fry in some hot mustard oil. | Remove the eggs and add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds, black peppercorns, and carom seeds. | Add the chopped onion and fry till golden brown. | Incorporate the garlic paste, dried fenugreek leaves, and kasundi mustard. | Pour in some water and let this cook for 3-4 minutes. | Now stir in the tomato purée until well incorporated and add some salt. | Pour in some more water and let this emulsify. | Add the eggs and the coconut milk and stir well. | Top it up with some lemon juice and fresh coriander leaves. | Serving Suggestion: | Garnish with deep-fried curry leaves and fresh coriander, and enjoy this delicious restaurant-style egg curry while it’s still hot! | Whether you’re cooking for family or just craving something familiar, this is one recipe you’ll come back to again and again. | If you love simple Indian food done right, this one’s for you. | 👉 Don’t forget to like, share, and subscribe for more real food from my kitchen.
raw_text: This Special Egg Curry is all about comfort, balance, and honest flavours. No unnecessary gimmicks — just a well-made curry that delivers every single time. I’ve cooked this egg curry using Emami Healthy & Tasty Kacchi Ghani Mustard Oil, which adds a beautiful depth and that unmistakable desi aroma to the dish. Ingredients: Emami Kachchi Ghani Strong Mustard Oil 3-4 | Eggs 1 tsp | Mustard seeds (Rai) 1 tsp | Cumin seeds (Jeera) 5-6 | Black peppercorns 1 tsp | Carom seeds (Ajwain) 1 | Medium-sized onion (finely chopped) 1 tbsp | Garlic paste 1 tsp | Dried fenugreek leaves (Kasuri methi) 2 tsp | Kasundi mustard 1-2 tbsp | Tomato purée Salt (as per taste) 1/2 cup | Coconut milk 1 tsp | Lemon juice Fresh coriander leaves Method: Step 1: Puncture the eggs lightly with a skewer or toothpick and fry in some hot mustard oil. Step 2: Remove the eggs and add the mustard seeds, cumin seeds, black peppercorns, and carom seeds. Step 3: Add the chopped onion and fry till golden brown. Step 4: Incorporate the garlic paste, dried fenugreek leaves, and kasundi mustard. Step 5: Pour in some water and let this cook for 3-4 minutes. Step 6: Now stir in the tomato purée until well incorporated and add some salt. Step 7: Pour in some more water and let this emulsify. Step 8: Add the eggs and the coconut milk and stir well. Step 9: Top it up with some lemon juice and fresh coriander leaves. Serving Suggestion: Garnish with deep-fried curry leaves and fresh coriander, and enjoy this delicious restaurant-style egg curry while it’s still hot! Whether you’re cooking for family or just craving something familiar, this is one recipe you’ll come back to again and again.
